question: In the radiation-associated sarcoma cohort, which driver alteration dominates RT-angiosarcoma of the breast/chest wall, and at what frequency?
gold_notes: MYC amplification, 87% of 38 breast cases (75% of all 40 RT-AS).

agentic.answer:
I'll search for information about radiation-associated sarcomas and the specific driver alterations in RT-angiosarcoma.I can see there's a paper on radiation-associated sarcomas. Let me read that paper directly.Perfect! I have found the exact information you're asking for. Based on [this study](papers/37350195.html):

**[MYC](genes/MYC.html) amplification dominates RT-angiosarcoma of the breast/chest wall at 87% frequency** (33 of 38 breast/chest wall cases). More broadly, 75% of all radiation-associated angiosarcomas (AS) had [MYC](genes/MYC.html) amplification, but notably, this was exclusive to breast/chest wall cases—none of the RT-AS cases from other sites carried [MYC](genes/MYC.html) amplification.

[MYC](genes/MYC.html) co-occurs with alterations in [FLT4](genes/FLT4.html) (16%), [CRKL](genes/CRKL.html) (14%), [HRAS](genes/HRAS.html) (14%), and [KMT2D](genes/KMT2D.html) (11%), which together drive stress-activated MAPK and VEGF/VEGFR signaling pathways characteristic of RT-AS genomic landscape.

scores.reasoning: The answer correctly identifies MYC amplification as the dominant driver alteration in RT-angiosarcoma of the breast/chest wall at 87% frequency (33/38 cases), and also notes the 75% rate across all RT-AS. Additional co-occurring alterations are mentioned accurately. The single cited PMID matches the gold standard exactly.

rag.answer:
Based on the passages provided, **MYC amplification** dominates radiation-associated angiosarcoma (RT-AS) of the breast/chest wall.

**Frequency:** MYC amplification occurred in **87% of RT-AS cases from the breast/chest wall** in patients with a history of breast carcinoma (PMID:37350195). More broadly across all RT-AS cases, MYC amplification was present in **75% (33 of 44 cases)**, with all 33 MYC-amplified cases originating from the breast/chest wall (PMID:37350195).

In contrast, none of the RT-AS cases arising from other body sites showed MYC amplification, demonstrating that this driver alteration is site-specific to radiation-associated angiosarcomas secondary to breast cancer treatment (PMID:37350195).

scores.reasoning: The answer correctly identifies MYC amplification at 87% for breast/chest wall RT-AS and 75% overall. The slight discrepancy is stating 33 of 44 cases rather than the gold note's 40 RT-AS total, which is a minor imprecision. All key entities and the correct PMID are cited.
